Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 01 - Su.file: Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 01 - Super Smash Bros. (3DS, GC, N64, Switch, Wii, Wii U) (gamerip) (1999).json

Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 02 - Su.file: Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 02 - Super Mario (3DS, GC, N64, Switch, Wii, Wii U) (gamerip) (1999).json
Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 02 - Su.notes: With over 100 tracks, Super Mario is of course very well represented in smash. So much so that it's almost overflowing with shit main themes from spin-off titles like Mario & Luigi and Mario Tennis. But if you can look past them, and the 15+ Main Theme remixes, you can find some banger remixes.

Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 03 - Ma.file: Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 03 - Mario Kart (Wii, 3DS, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (2008).json

Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 04 - Do.file: Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 04 - Donkey Kong (3DS, GC, N64, Switch, Wii, Wii U) (gamerip) (1999).json
Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 04 - Do.notes: I love Donkey Kong music and although I would have loved for more, I feel like some of these remixes are so fucking good. Over smash history they have included an outrageous 8 versions of Jungle Level, but when they have diversified, they've smashed it. Stickerbush Symphony, Crocodile Cacophony and Map/Bonus Level are all so fucking good.

Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 05 - Th.file: Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 05 - The Legend of Zelda (N64, Wii, 3DS, GC, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (1999).json
Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 05 - Th.notes: Zelda, arguably one of the best Nintendo franchises soundtrack-wise, is not very well represented in the smash remixes front. There's a lot of generic this and that. Although that's not to say there aren't diamonds in the rough. The Molgera remix is one of my favourites in the whole game!

Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 06 - Me.file: Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 06 - Metroid (N64, Wii, 3DS, GC, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (1999).json

Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 07 - Yo.file: Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 07 - Yoshi (N64, Wii, 3DS, GC, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (1999).json
Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 07 - Yo.notes: Sadly, Yoshi's Story became a legal stage in Ultimate, meaning everyone has played these songs to death. I get it, but I love Yoshi music and they will forever remain in my heart, even if I've heard them too much. Yoshi's Story was a big game for me as a child and I just love it so there. I can't forgive the child-like off-key whistling in many of these songs though.

Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 16 - Wa.file: Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 16 - WarioWare (3DS, Switch, Wii, Wii U) (gamerip) (2008).json
Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 16 - Wa.notes: Tragically, Wario games have been treated terribly when it comes to smash. The entire rich history of Wario Land has been erased, despite them adding a costume for Wario and changing his victory theme. There is so much lost potential there, it hurts. WarioWare itself is represented OK, but there was nothing new in Ultimate which makes me sad. At least the strength of the original Brawl remixes carries us on. But my heart breaks for Wario Land.

Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 17 - Pi.file: Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 17 - Pikmin (Wii, 3DS, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (2008).json
Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 17 - Pi.notes: Pikmin music is something that I feel very strongly about. It's got a very special, wacky, extraterrestrial vibe, which is reflected in its wheep-whoop instrumentation and funky harmonies. It's also above all a peaceful game with peaceful music. I can imagine it's very hard to translate that into engaging battle music, but at least a couple of the remixes are solid. Otherwise, I have to say the spirit of Pikmin isn't as gorgeously represented in Smash as I would have liked. I could have done with more, and embracing the weird a bit more.

Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 18 - An.file: Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 18 - Animal Crossing (Wii, 3DS, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (2008).json

Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 19 - Wi.file: Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 19 - Wii Fit (3DS, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (2014).json
Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 19 - Wi.notes: You'd never think this would happen but they really went above and beyond for Wii Fit. I wouldn't have known how much the original soundtrack slaps if it weren't for some of these excellent remixes. I got so excited by how good they are, I put them together in a small mix which can you download on this very website! The arrangers really did a great job with capturing the Nintendo Wii vibe and making them fast-paced for battle. Same goes for Wii Sports.

Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 20 - Pu.file: Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 20 - Punch-Out!! (3DS, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (2014).json
Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 20 - Pu.notes: Smash Ultimate really did Punch Out dirty by not putting in any new music. These measly 5 tracks all sound the same and are super plain. I'm not saying that Punch Out is a wealth of great source material but they could have made more of an effort. Maybe even put in some original NES music, this is all from the Wii game.

Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 29 - Ba.file: Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 29 - Bayonetta (3DS, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (2016).json
Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 29 - Ba.notes: Bayonetta music is something special. It sounds like nothing else. It really captures the sexy over the top vibes of the game perfectly and I'm honestly so impressed. In smash they have a good selection of original tracks, and two remixes which only really swap out vocals because I guess they couldn't get the rights to the singer. It's fine, this music really doesn't need much remixing, they already carry the full weight of Bayonetta's drama and class with them. I'm obsessed. And I'm pissed that they added literally nothing new in Ultimate!

Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 33 - Dr.file: Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 33 - Dragon Quest (Switch) (gamerip) (2019).json
Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 33 - Dr.notes: Dragon Quest music was only ever composed by this one dude who made classical music with an orchestra first, and then translated it to chiptunes. Honestly, I respect that, and it means that you play a remake of any Dragon Quest game today and you're straight up listening to classical music as you play, which is in itself a whole vibe. For smash though, the guy let them use one adventure and one battle theme from each game and said that'll do. They all kinda sound the same, there are no fun remixes, and anyone who has played the games will be immediately bored of them because there is so little variation in the games.

Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 34 - Ba.file: Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 34 - Banjo-Kazooie (Switch, Switch 2) (gamerip) (2019).json

Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 36 - AR.file: Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 36 - ARMS (Switch) (gamerip) (2020).json

Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 37 - Mi.file: Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 37 - Minecraft (Switch) (gamerip) (2020).json
Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 37 - Mi.notes: Minecraft was always going to be a struggle. It's mostly ambient music so how on earth are you meant to make battle remixes for smash? Sakurai attempts to solve this problem by hiring his very best remixers to tackle obscure-ass tracks from Minecraft Dungeons and the minigames from the discontinued console editions of the Minecraft. There's no actual music from Minecraft. I really think they could have gone for Stal or Sweden or something for jokes, or Lena Raine. I think there was potential there but this selection is shit because the source material is so weak.
